body:
## Context
`settings.html` is a static settings page with two checkbox controls (Notifications, Email digest). Their state is persisted to `localStorage` by `src/settings.js`. Styling lives in `src/styles.css`.

## Goal
Add a **dark-mode toggle** to the settings page that lets the user switch the page between light and dark appearance, and persists that choice across reloads (consistent with how the existing toggles persist to `localStorage`).

## Acceptance criteria
- A clearly-labelled dark-mode control appears in the `#settings` section of `settings.html`.
- Toggling it switches the page to a dark color scheme immediately (no reload needed).
- The choice is persisted to `localStorage` and re-applied on page load.
- The existing Notifications / Email digest toggles continue to work unchanged.
- Implementation stays consistent with the existing vanilla-HTML/CSS/JS style (no new framework or build step).

## Notes
This repo is a static app with **no test harness** (no `package.json`, no CI, no test files). The plan must address how the change will be verified per the team's automated-verification standard.
title: Add a dark-mode toggle to the settings page
